steps.title: Interpret what the ratio 1:18 means
steps.content:
A ratio of mentors to participants of 1:18 means: for every 1 mentor, there are 18 participants. In other words, the number of participants is always 18 times the number of mentors:
$$\text{participants} = 18 \times \text{mentors}$$

steps.title: Apply the relationship with x mentors
steps.content:
Since there are $x$ mentors at the robotics camp, substitute into the relationship:
$$\text{participants} = 18x$$
That is the correct answer.
